262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	            # Emit a structured warning too (#K-OBS-2): a tracker call failing used to be recorded
326	            # ONLY to the trail, so e.g. a provenance-artifact serialization failure dropped the
327	            # #G-009 artifact silently — an operator auditing the run saw no artifact and no reason.
328	            _log.warning(
329	                "experiment tracker call failed (%s: %s); this metric/artifact was not logged",
330	                type(exc).__name__,
331	                exc,
332	            )
333	
334	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
335	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
336	        decision = gate_handler(review)
337	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
338	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
339	        trail.record(
340	            "gate",
341	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
342	            gate=review.gate,
343	            approved=approved,
344	            reason=reason,
345	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
346	        )
347	        if not approved:
348	            raise GateError(
349	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
350	            )
351	
352	    trail.record(
353	        "run_start",
354	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
355	        study=defn.name,
356	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
357	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
358	    )
359	    if tracker is not None:
360	        try:
361	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
362	                defn.name,
363	                {
364	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
365	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
366	                },
367	            )
368	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
369	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
370	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
371	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
372	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
373	            _log.warning(
374	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
375	                "to the tracker for this study",
376	                type(exc).__name__,
377	                exc,
378	            )
379	
380	    ok = False
381	    started_at = time.monotonic()
382	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
383	    try:
384	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
385	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
386	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
387	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
388	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
389	        bootstrap_tracing()
390	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
391	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
392	        # ---- INGEST ----
393	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
394	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
395	            try:
396	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
397	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
398	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
399	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
400	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
401	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
402	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
403	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
404	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
405	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
406	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
407	                raise IngestionError(
408	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
409	                )
410	            if not corpus.units:
411	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
412	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
413	            source_version: str | None = None
414	            if versioner is not None:
415	                # The versioner is an optional seam; a storage failure (PermissionError,
416	                # OSError) must surface as the documented IngestionError, not a raw untyped
417	                # exception that the caller cannot distinguish from a fatal firewall breach
418	                # (pass 3, #F-008). Versioning is part of ingesting the source, so a failure to
419	                # content-address it is an ingestion failure.
420	                try:
421	                    source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
422	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize optional-seam storage failures
423	                    raise IngestionError(
424	                        f"data versioner failed to store {plan.source!r}: {exc}"
425	                    ) from exc
426	            trail.record(
427	                "ingest",
428	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
429	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
430	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
431	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
432	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
433	                source_version=source_version,
434	            )
435	            # SLO-relevant scale metrics to the tracker so corpus-size↔latency regressions are
436	            # analysable from the experiment store, not only the provenance trail (pass 3,
437	            # #F-029). Best-effort via track() (no-ops if no tracker/run_id).
438	            track(_count_logger("n_units", float(len(corpus.units))))
439	            track(_count_logger("n_relations", float(len(corpus.relations))))
440	            # Compute the corpus unit-id set ONCE and reuse it for both the split-id and feature-id
441	            # validations (pass 4, #G-023 — was built twice on the same corpus).
442	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
443	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
444	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
445	                assert plan.split is not None
446	                unknown = (
447	                    set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)
448	                ) - corpus_unit_ids
449	                if unknown:
450	                    raise FirewallViolation(
451	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
452	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
453	                    )
454	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
455	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
456	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
457	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
458	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
459	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
460	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
461	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
462	                    raise FirewallViolation(
463	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
464	                    )
465	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
466	                if ghost:
467	                    raise FirewallViolation(
468	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
469	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
470	                    )
471	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
472	                trail.record(
473	                    "features",
474	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
475	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
476	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
477	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
478	                )
479	
480	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
481	        baseline_instant = _now()
482	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
483	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
484	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
485	            with guard.sealed():
486	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
487	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
488	                raise FirewallViolation(
489	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
490	                    "was not built from this corpus"
491	                )
492	            trail.record(
493	                "baseline",
494	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
495	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
496	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
497	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
498	            )
499	
500	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
501	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
502	
503	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
504	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
505	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
506	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
507	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
508	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
509	
510	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
511	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
512	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
513	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
514	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
515	                if not hypotheses:
516	                    # discover() surfaced nothing — a vacuous run indistinguishable from a broken
517	                    # callable that forgot to return. Fail loud (pass 3, #F-042).
518	                    raise FirewallViolation(
519	                        "discover() returned no hypotheses — a discovery run with zero "
520	                        "hypotheses is vacuous and cannot produce a confirmatory verdict"
521	                    )
522	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
523	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
524	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
525	                trail.record(
526	                    "discovery",
527	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
528	                    n=len(hypotheses),
529	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
530	                )
531	
532	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
533	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
534	                if not held_out.units:
535	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
536	                preregister_instant = _now()
537	                for h in hypotheses:
538	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
539	                        raise FirewallViolation(
540	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
541	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
542	                        )
543	                    vt = require_preregistered(
544	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
545	                    )
546	                    trail.record(
547	                        "preregister",
548	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
549	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
550	                        digest=vt.digest,
551	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
552	                    )
553	                run_gate(
554	                    GateReview(
555	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
556	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
557	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
558	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
559	                        payload={
560	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
561	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
562	                        },
563	                    )
564	                )
565	
566	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
567	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
568	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
569	                for h in hypotheses:
570	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
571	                    # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing
572	                    # verdict.hypothesis_id would raise an opaque AttributeError instead of the
573	                    # documented typed error (pass 3, #F-021). Guard the contract explicitly.
574	                    if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
575	                        raise FirewallViolation(
576	                            f"confirm_held_out returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
577	                            f"{h.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
578	                        )
579	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
580	                        raise FirewallViolation(
581	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
582	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
583	                        )
584	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
585	                    trail.record(
586	                        "verdict",
587	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
588	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
589	                        label=verdict.label.value,
590	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
591	                    )
592	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
593	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
594	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
595	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
596	
597	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
598	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
599	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
600	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
601	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
602	            if not claim_records:
603	                raise ValueError(
604	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
605	                )
606	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
607	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
608	            track(_count_logger("n_claims", float(len(claim_records))))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
609	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
610	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
611	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
612	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
613	            # Enforce — do not merely *record* — the fingerprint agreement (pass 3, #F-001).
614	            # Pass-2 #137 made the engine fingerprint authoritative and surfaced the match as a
615	            # boolean in the gate payload, but a non-interactive gate (auto_approve) approves
616	            # regardless, so a source whose claim_fingerprint() lies about a claim set that
617	            # claims() does not actually yield completed a run with the discrepancy silently
618	            # recorded. The source's self-report is a content commitment for pre-registration;
619	            # a disagreement means the scored claims are not the set the source attested to —
620	            # a provenance-integrity breach the engine must refuse, not defer to a human.
621	            if source_claim_fp != engine_claim_fp:
622	                raise FirewallViolation(
623	                    "claims source self-reported claim_fingerprint "
624	                    f"{source_claim_fp!r} but the engine computed {engine_claim_fp!r} over the "
625	                    "claims actually yielded — the scored claim set does not match the source's "
626	                    "attested fingerprint (provenance-integrity breach)"
627	                )
628	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
629	            run_gate(
630	                GateReview(
631	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
632	                    frm=prev_phase,
633	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
634	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
635	                    payload={
636	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
637	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
638	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
639	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
640	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
641	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
642	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
643	                    },
644	                )
645	            )
646	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
647	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
648	
649	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
650	                    trail.record(
651	                        "preregister",
652	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
653	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
654	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
655	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
656	                    )
657	                    trail.record(
658	                        "verdict",
659	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
660	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
661	                        label=v.label.value,
662	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
663	                    )
664	
665	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
666	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
667	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
668	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
669	                    baseline,
670	                    claim_records,
671	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
672	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
673	                    authority=plan.authority,
674	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
675	                    source_name=defn.name,
676	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
677	                )
678	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
679	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
680	                trail.record(
681	                    "score",
682	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
683	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
684	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
685	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
686	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
687	                )
688	                sc = scorecard
689	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
690	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
691	                track(
692	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
693	                )
694	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
695	                if ar is not None:
696	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
697	
698	        # ---- REPORT ----
699	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
700	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
701	            trail.record(
702	                "report",
703	                "assembled reproducibility package",
704	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
705	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
706	            )
707	
708	        if tuple(visited) != required:
709	            raise FirewallViolation(
710	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
711	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
712	            )
713	        if verify_trail:
714	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
715	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
716	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
717	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
718	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
719	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
720	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
721	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
722	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
723	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
724	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
725	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
726	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
727	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
728	        trail.record(
729	            "run_end",
730	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
731	            study=defn.name,
732	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
733	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
734	        )
735	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
736	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
737	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
738	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
739	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
740	
741	            def _log_trail(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
742	                import json
743	                import os
744	                import tempfile
745	
746	                from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
747	
748	                # Serialize FIRST, with a JSON-safe fallback (#K-OBS-2). freeze() accepts any
749	                # HASHABLE leaf, so a payload may carry a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes,
750	                # datetime, Enum) — e.g. in gate evidence. The old code called json.dump INSIDE the
751	                # NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) block BEFORE `path = fh.name`, so a TypeError
752	                # there (a) orphaned the temp file (cleanup unreachable — path never assigned) AND
753	                # (b) was swallowed by track() so the #G-009 artifact silently never attached.
754	                # Building the string up front with default=str means serialization can't leak a
755	                # file and the artifact still attaches even with such values.
756	                payload = json.dumps(
757	                    list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), indent=2, default=str
758	                )
759	                fh = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
760	                    "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
761	                )
762	                # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
763	                # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
764	                # (#H-013). `fh.name` is always assigned (the file exists once created), so the
765	                # finally cleanup is always reachable. The tracker copies synchronously.
766	                try:
767	                    fh.write(payload)
768	                    fh.close()
769	                    t.log_artifact(rid, fh.name)
770	                finally:
771	                    try:
772	                        os.unlink(fh.name)
773	                    except OSError:
774	                        pass
775	
776	            track(_log_trail)
777	        ok = True
778	    except Exception as exc:
779	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
780	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
781	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
782	        try:
783	            trail.record(
784	                "run_failed",
785	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
786	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
787	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
788	                last_phase=last_phase,
789	            )
790	        except Exception as inner:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
791	            # The run_failed entry could not be recorded (payload unserializable, clock raised,
792	            # trail lock contended). Don't silently swallow — emit a structured warning so the
793	            # failure reason is at least in the logs, even though it is absent from the trail
794	            # (pass 3, #F-030). The original exception is still re-raised below.
795	            _log.warning(
796	                "could not record run_failed provenance entry (%s: %s)",
797	                type(inner).__name__,
798	                inner,
799	            )
800	        raise
801	    finally:
802	        trace_ctx.close()
803	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
804	            try:
805	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
806	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
807	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
808	                pass
809	
810	    return StudyResult(
811	        study=defn.name,
812	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
813	        baseline=baseline,
814	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
815	        scorecard=scorecard,
816	        phases=tuple(visited),
817	        provenance=trail.entries,
818	        source_version=source_version,
819	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
820	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
821	    )
822

content.content:
1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	
219	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
220	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
221	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
222	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
223	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
224	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
225	    """
226	    return tuple(
227	        {
228	            "seq": e.seq,
229	            "kind": e.kind,
230	            "summary": e.summary,
231	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
232	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
233	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
234	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
235	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
236	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
237	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
238	        }
239	        for e in entries
240	    )
241	
242	
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
260	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
261	            raise ProvenanceError(
262	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
263	            )
264	        prev = e.entry_hash
265	
266	
267	def from_records(
268	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
269	    *,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
272	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
273	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
274	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
275	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
276	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
277	        if missing:
278	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
279	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
280	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
281	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
282	        rebuilt.append(
283	            ProvenanceEntry(
284	                seq=r["seq"],
285	                kind=r["kind"],
286	                summary=r["summary"],
287	                payload=r["payload"],
288	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
289	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
290	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
291	            )
292	        )
293	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
294	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
295	    return entries
296

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior). Read fresh on each bootstrap (#F-033) so
59	# an env var set after import still takes effect; the module constant is the default + the value
60	# tests monkeypatch.
61	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
62	
63	
64	def _migration_lock_timeout_ms() -> int:
65	    """The advisory-lock wait bound, re-read from the env each call (#F-033).
66	
67	    Falls back to the import-time module constant when the env var is unset, so a test that
68	    monkeypatches ``_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`` still drives the value.
69	    """
70	    raw = os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS")
71	    if raw is None:
72	        return _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS
73	    return max(0, int(raw))
74	
75	
76	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
77	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
78	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
79	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
80	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
81	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
82	
83	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
84	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
85	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
86	
87	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
88	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
89	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
90	
91	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
92	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
93	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
94	_atexit_registered = False
95	
96	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
97	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
98	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
99	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
100	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
101	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
102	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
103	
104	
105	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
107	
108	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
109	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
110	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
111	    """
112	    try:
113	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
114	        host = u.hostname or ""
115	        port = u.port
116	        scheme = u.scheme
117	        path = u.path
118	    except ValueError:
119	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
120	    if not host:
121	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
122	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
123	        host = f"[{host}]"
124	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
125	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
126	
127	
128	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
129	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
130	
131	    Layers, in order of precision:
132	
133	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
134	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
135	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
136	       *other* URI in the text;
137	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
138	
139	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
140	    not pass as ``conn_str``.
141	
142	    Precision caveat (pass 5, #H-023): layer 1 is an EXACT-substring replacement, so it scrubs the
143	    primary connection string precisely only when that string appears verbatim in ``text``. If a
144	    backend reformats it (re-quoting, reordering DSN keywords, percent-encoding the password) the
145	    verbatim match misses and only the layers-2–3 regex backstops apply — which still strip
146	    ``://user:pass@`` userinfo and ``password=`` tokens, but are not guaranteed exhaustive for
147	    every backend message shape. It is "precise when the conn string appears verbatim", not
148	    "always precise".
149	    """
150	    if conn_str:
151	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
152	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
153	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
154	
155	
156	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
157	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
158	
159	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
160	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
161	    """
162	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
163	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
164	
165	
166	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
167	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
168	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
169	    that can raise)."""
170	    try:
171	        pool.close()
172	    except Exception:
173	        pass
174	
175	
176	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
177	    # Snapshot under the meta-lock before iterating (#F-020): _OPEN_POOLS is appended under
178	    # _init_lock elsewhere, and on a free-threaded (GIL-less) build a concurrent list.append
179	    # reallocation during iteration is undefined behavior. _safe_close never takes _init_lock, so
180	    # closing outside the lock cannot deadlock.
181	    with _init_lock:
182	        pools = list(_OPEN_POOLS)
183	    for p in pools:
184	        _safe_close(p)
185	
186	
187	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
188	    global _atexit_registered
189	    with _init_lock:
190	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
191	        if not _atexit_registered:
192	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
193	            _atexit_registered = True
194	
195	
196	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
197	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
198	    with _init_lock:
199	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
200	        if lock is None:
201	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
202	        return lock
203	
204	
205	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
206	    with _init_lock:
207	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
208	
209	
210	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
211	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
212	
213	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
214	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
215	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
216	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
217	    """
218	    timeout_ms = _migration_lock_timeout_ms()
219	    if timeout_ms:
220	        conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(timeout_ms),))
221	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
222	
223	
224	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
225	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
226	
227	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
228	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
229	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
230	    or the extra is not installed.
231	    """
232	    try:
233	        if use_memory:
234	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
235	
236	            return MemorySaver()
237	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
238	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
239	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
240	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
241	        raise RuntimeError(
242	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
243	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
244	        ) from exc
245	
246	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
247	
248	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
249	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
250	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
251	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
252	    if cached is not None:
253	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
254	
255	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
256	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
257	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
258	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
259	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
260	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
261	        if cached is not None:
262	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
263	
264	        failure: str | None = None
265	        pool: Any = None
266	        try:
267	            # Pool bounds are env-tunable (#F-040): the reasoning seam already runs 8 worker
268	            # threads, so a fixed max_size=8 can be saturated under concurrent reasoning+checkpoint
269	            # load on larger hardware. connect_timeout bounds an individual TCP connect so a
270	            # black-holed host fails fast instead of hanging bootstrap for the OS default.
271	            min_size = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "1")))
272	            max_size = max(min_size or 1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "8")))
273	            connect_timeout = max(1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "10")))
274	            pool = ConnectionPool(
275	                conn_str,
276	                min_size=min_size,
277	                max_size=max_size,
278	                kwargs={
279	                    "autocommit": True,
280	                    "prepare_threshold": 0,
281	                    "row_factory": dict_row,
282	                    "connect_timeout": connect_timeout,
283	                },
284	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
285	                open=True,
286	            )
287	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
288	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
289	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
290	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
291	            with pool.connection() as conn:
292	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
293	                try:
294	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
295	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
296	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
297	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
298	                finally:
299	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
300	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
301	                    try:
302	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
303	                    except Exception:
304	                        pass
305	                    # Reset the session lock_timeout GUC (#G-002): the autocommit connection set it
306	                    # at SESSION scope for the bootstrap, then returns to the pool and is reused for
307	                    # all later checkpoint reads/writes — which would otherwise silently inherit a
308	                    # non-default lock_timeout the operator never configured for normal ops.
309	                    try:
310	                        conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")
311	                    except Exception:
312	                        pass
313	            with _init_lock:
314	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
315	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
316	        except Exception as exc:
317	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
318	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
319	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
320	            failure = (
321	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
322	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
323	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
324	            )
325	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
326	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
327	            if pool is not None:
328	                _safe_close(pool)
329	                with _init_lock:
330	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
331	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
332	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
333	
334	
335	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
336	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
337	
338	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
339	    otherwise Postgres.
340	    """
341	    if use_memory is None:
342	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
343	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
344

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended. (Honest scope, pass 5 #H-012: this no-op-on-re-entry holds only when the
25	  prior ``approved`` decision is present in the ``gate_decisions`` graph state the node reads;
26	  it is idempotency over the durable LangGraph state, not a guard against a caller fabricating
27	  state.)
28	
29	Recorder is OPTIONAL here, deliberately asymmetric with ``run_study`` (pass 5, #H-011): the
30	runner OWNS the provenance trail and records every gate decision to it unconditionally, whereas
31	the graph node persists decisions in LangGraph checkpoint state and the ``recorder`` only BRIDGES
32	them into the hash-chained trail. A GOVERNANCE-§3-compliant run must pass a recorder (omitting it
33	warns — see :func:`make_gate_node`); it is optional only so a study not using the engine trail can
34	still drive the graph. The trail write, when a recorder is present, happens before the state update
35	is returned but the two are not a single atomic transaction — a recorder failure raises rather
36	than half-applying (#G-020).
37	
38	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
39	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
40	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
41	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
42	extra, imported lazily).
43	"""
44	
45	from __future__ import annotations
46	
47	import warnings
48	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
49	
50	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
51	
52	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
53	
54	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
55	
56	
57	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
58	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
59	
60	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
61	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
62	    """
63	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
64	        raise GateError(
65	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
66	        )
67	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
68	    if target != gate.name:
69	        raise GateError(
70	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
71	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
72	        )
73	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
74	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
75	        raise GateError(
76	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
77	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
78	        )
79	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
80	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
81	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
82	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
83	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
84	
85	
86	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
87	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
88	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
89	
90	
91	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
92	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
93	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
94	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
95	            return True
96	    return False
97	
98	
99	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
100	
101	
102	def make_gate_node(
103	    gate: Gate,
104	    propose: GateProposal,
105	    *,
106	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
107	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
108	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
109	
110	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
111	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
112	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
113	
114	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
115	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
116	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
117	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
118	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
119	
120	    Omitting ``recorder`` is a GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap: gate decisions then live only in
121	    (non-tamper-evident) LangGraph state, never in the hash-chained trail, so a checkpoint edit
122	    flipping ``rejected``→``approved`` is undetectable by ``verify_records``. Because that
123	    insecure configuration is effortless to ship by accident, building a node without a recorder
124	    emits a warning rather than failing silently (pass 3, #F-007).
125	    """
126	    if recorder is None:
127	        warnings.warn(
128	            f"make_gate_node({gate.name!r}) built with no recorder: gate decisions will NOT be "
129	            "written to the hash-chained provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap) — pass a "
130	            "recorder that calls trail.record('gate', ...) for a tamper-evident audit trail",
131	            stacklevel=2,
132	        )
133	
134	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
135	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
136	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
137	        try:
138	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
139	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
140	            raise RuntimeError(
141	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
142	            ) from exc
143	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
144	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
145	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
146	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
147	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
148	        while True:
149	            resumed = interrupt(current)
150	            try:
151	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
152	            except GateError as exc:
153	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
154	                continue
155	            if recorder is not None:
156	                # Record to the trail BEFORE returning the state update (#G-020): the validated
157	                # decision must land in the hash-chained trail and the graph state atomically. If
158	                # the recorder fails, surface a clear GateError INSTEAD of returning the update —
159	                # so the decision is neither half-applied (in graph state but absent from the
160	                # tamper-evident trail) nor silently lost; the operator/runner sees an explicit
161	                # failure to durably record the approval rather than a quietly diverging trail.
162	                try:
163	                    for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
164	                        recorder(rec)
165	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize any recorder backend failure
166	                    raise GateError(
167	                        f"gate {gate.name!r}: decision validated but could NOT be recorded to the "
168	                        f"provenance trail ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}); not applying the state "
169	                        "update so graph state cannot diverge from the trail"
170	                    ) from exc
171	            return update
172	
173	    return node

1	"""Data versioning — content-addressed, local, deterministic (METHODOLOGY.md §7, ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every finding must cite the exact bytes it was computed from. This provides a dependency-free,
4	on-box content-addressing store: an artifact is hashed (SHA-256) and copied under a local
5	store keyed by that hash, so the same bytes always yield the same version id and any artifact
6	can be re-fetched by id. (The prior platform used DVC via an external tool; this keeps the
7	engine self-contained — an adapter may swap in DVC by implementing the same Protocol.)
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import hashlib
13	import os
14	import re
15	import shutil
16	import tempfile
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Protocol
19	
20	_CHUNK = 1 << 20  # 1 MiB
21	_SHA256_HEX = re.compile(r"[0-9a-f]{64}")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class DataVersioner(Protocol):
26	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
27	        """Version the artifact at ``path``; return its content hash / version id."""
28	        ...
29	
30	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
31	        """Return the content hash of ``path`` without storing it."""
32	        ...
33	
34	
35	def _hash_file(path: Path) -> str:
36	    h = hashlib.sha256()
37	    with path.open("rb") as f:
38	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(_CHUNK), b""):
39	            h.update(chunk)
40	    return h.hexdigest()
41	
42	
43	class LocalDataVersioner:
44	    """Content-addressed local artifact store. Deterministic and fully offline.
45	
46	    ``store_dir`` defaults to ``ASSAY_DATA_STORE`` or ``./.assay-data``. Files are stored under
47	    ``<store>/<aa>/<full-hash>`` (sharded by the first two hex chars).
48	    """
49	
50	    def __init__(self, store_dir: str | None = None) -> None:
51	        root = store_dir or os.environ.get("ASSAY_DATA_STORE") or str(Path.cwd() / ".assay-data")
52	        self._root = Path(root)
53	
54	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
55	        p = Path(path)
56	        if not p.is_file():
57	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"cannot version a non-file path: {path!r}")
58	        return _hash_file(p)
59	
60	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
61	        """Store the artifact under its content hash; return the hash (version id).
62	
63	        Idempotent: storing the same bytes twice is a no-op returning the same id. Publish is
64	        atomic — content is copied to a *per-writer-unique* temp file (so two processes
65	        storing the same digest never clobber a shared temp) and then ``os.replace``-d into
66	        place, so a reader never observes a partial artifact. The store is content-addressed,
67	        so an interrupted/duplicate write is harmless.
68	        """
69	        digest = self.fingerprint(path)
70	        dest = self._root / digest[:2] / digest
71	        if dest.exists():
72	            return digest
73	        dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
74	        fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(dest.parent), prefix=".tmp-")
75	        os.close(fd)
76	        tmp = Path(tmp_name)
77	        try:
78	            shutil.copy2(path, tmp)
79	            os.replace(tmp, dest)  # atomic publish within the same filesystem
80	        finally:
81	            tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True)  # no-op once replaced; cleans up on copy failure
82	        return digest
83	
84	    def path_for(self, digest: str) -> Path:
85	        """Local path of a previously-stored artifact by its version id.
86	
87	        The version id MUST be a SHA-256 hex digest (the only form :meth:`put` ever returns).
88	        Validating it rejects path-traversal payloads (e.g. ``../../etc/passwd``) that would
89	        otherwise compose a path outside the store (#112).
90	        """
91	        if not _SHA256_HEX.fullmatch(digest):
92	            raise ValueError(
93	                f"invalid version id {digest!r} (expected a 64-char sha256 hex digest)"
94	            )
95	        return self._root / digest[:2] / digest

4	name; the engine looks it up to run. This is the only place adapters and the engine meet by
